question: Is the museum gallery interior (architecture, walls, lighting) rendered in an Art Deco style (e.g., geometric patterns, sleek lines)?
Is there a female portrait hanging on the left wall rendered in a Pop Art style?
Does the Pop Art portrait on the left display a prominent Ben-Day dot printing effect?
Is there a water lily landscape painting hanging on the right wall rendered in an Impressionist style?
Does the Impressionist painting on the right exhibit visible brushstrokes characteristic of the style?
Is there an abstract sculpture on a pedestal in the center rendered in a Cubist style (e.g., geometric fragmentation)?
Is the elderly visitor rendered in a watercolor style (e.g., fluid edges, translucent washes)?
Is the watercolor style of the elderly visitor visually distinct from the surrounding Art Deco environment?
Does the elderly visitor have a cane?
Is the visitor leaning forward and partially obscuring the Cubist sculpture?
Is there an ornate gold-framed mirror on the back wall rendered in a Baroque style?
Does the reflection inside the mirror depict a Surrealist miniature scene?
Does the Surrealist scene in the mirror contain floating eyes and intersecting staircases?
Are the various art styles (Pop Art, Impressionist, Cubist, Watercolor, Surrealism) clearly distinguishable from one another rather than blended into a uniform style?
